title: fraxinus rotundifolia subsp. persica
intro: Fraxinus rotundifolia subsp. persica, also known as Persian manna ash or Fraxinus excelsior subsp. rotundifolia, is a subspecies of the ash tree native to Europe, Asia, and Africa, characterized by its broad, rounded leaves and small, greenish-yellow flowers. The bark of the Fraxinus rotundifolia subsp. persica contains a sticky, sweet substance called manna, which has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including soothing respiratory issues, reducing fever, and treating digestive problems, thanks to its flavonoids and saponins content. Fraxinus rotundifolia subsp. persica can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine as a poultice or salve for topical applications.
